Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Jordan: Well, I mean, expectations, sure, I guess they play a role. It’s hard not to feel like there’s this invisible standard I’m supposed to meet, you know? But I don’t really talk about it with anyone. It’s like, I don’t want to burden people with my worries. I just keep it all bottled up. Sometimes I think it’s just me putting too much pressure on myself. I mean, I know I should probably lighten up, but it’s easier said than done, right? It’s just frustrating, and I don’t know if anyone really gets it.

topic_probe_events.prompt_context:
- Not belonging (Identity)

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Jordan: Well, I mean, belonging is a tricky thing, right? Sometimes I feel like I’m on the outside looking in, especially when it comes to those expectations. It’s like I’m trying to fit into this mold that I’m not sure I was ever meant to be in. I don’t know if it’s just me feeling that way or if there’s something to it. I guess I just want to feel like I’m doing enough, but then I wonder if I’m even measuring up to whatever it is I think I should be. It’s confusing, and I don’t really talk about it, so it just kind of lingers there. But, I mean, I’m sure everyone feels a bit out of place sometimes, right?

topic_probe_events.prompt_context:
- Pain or fatigue (Health)

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Jordan: Well, I mean, now that you mention it, I do notice that I get really tense sometimes, especially in those situations. Like, my shoulders feel tight, and I just want to kind of shrink into myself, you know? It’s like my body is reacting to all that pressure and anxiety. And then there are times when I just feel drained, like I’ve run a marathon or something, even though I haven’t really done anything physically demanding. It’s frustrating because I want to be engaged and present, but my body feels like it’s fighting against me. But, I guess it’s just part of how I handle things, right? Everyone has their own ways of coping, I suppose.

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Jordan: Well, I mean, my living situation is kind of... complicated, I guess. I’m in a small apartment, and it can feel pretty cramped at times. There’s not a lot of space to breathe, you know? I think that adds to the feeling of being overwhelmed. Sometimes, I just want to have a place where I can unwind without feeling like I’m on top of everything. But then again, I know it’s not the worst situation out there. I mean, I have a roof over my head, so I shouldn’t complain too much. It’s just that sometimes I feel like the environment around me doesn’t help with all the pressure I’m already feeling. But I’m sure others have it tougher, right?

topic_probe_events.prompt_context:
- Rumination pattern (Coping)

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Jordan: Well, I mean, yeah, that definitely resonates. It’s like my mind just starts racing, going over everything I didn’t get done or things I might have messed up. I can’t seem to turn it off, you know? It’s almost like I’m replaying conversations or scenarios in my head, trying to figure out if I said the right thing or if I missed something important. And then I get caught up in that cycle, which just makes everything feel heavier. It’s frustrating because I want to let go and relax, but my brain just won’t cooperate. I guess it’s just part of how I handle stress, but it can feel pretty relentless at times. But, I mean, I’m sure other people deal with similar things, right?

topic_probe_events.client_reply: Jordan: Well, I mean, yeah, that definitely fits. It’s like there’s this invisible pressure to perform, and if I don’t meet it, I feel like I’m letting everyone down, including myself. That thought just adds to the anxiety, you know? And then I start questioning if I’m really capable of handling things, which just makes it worse. I want to feel confident and in control, but instead, it feels like I’m just trying to keep my head above water. But, I mean, I’m sure other people feel that way too, right?
